methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $11.0B.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $11B

data.narrative:
Looking Glass is a Series A semiconductor company founded by Shawn Frayne that has raised $30M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 total private and public peers in the Chips & Semiconductors sector, including private-stage companies such as SambaNova Systems ($11.0B), Etched ($5.0B), and Positron AI ($1.0B), alongside public benchmarks including NVDA, AMD, and ALAB.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $30M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a typical 2.0x Series A step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $60M. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (SambaNova, Etched, Positron AI) yields a median valuation of $5.0B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $11.0B. The wide spread reflects heterogeneity in chip-stack companies' market positioning and capital efficiency at this stage.

These two methods produce a triangulated valuation range of $51M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median estimate of $2.5B. The substantial gap between stage-progression and peer-median implies reflects the outsized valuations commanded by differentiated chip architecture companies in the current environment. Key uncertainties include product differentiation relative to the peer set, path to revenue, and whether recent semiconductor capital dynamics persist. The median estimate of $2.5B should be weighted against execution risk and the specificity of Looking Glass's technical positioning within the peer cohort.
